prompt:
While researching a city composting program, a student took the following notes:
• Residents sort food scraps into green bins.
• The scraps become compost for public parks.
• The program cut landfill waste by a fifth.

The student wants to report the program's main result. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Since residents began sorting food scraps into green bins, the city has cut the waste sent to its landfill by a fifth.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to report the program's main result.

choices.text: Composting, the natural breakdown of organic matter into rich soil, has long been practiced by home gardeners.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the program's main result as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The compost made from the collected scraps is later spread across the soil of the city's many public parks.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the program's main result as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Across the city, residents now carefully sort their leftover food scraps into the green bins set out for collection.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the program's main result as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to report the program's main result; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a salt marsh carbon study, a student took the following notes:
• Marsh mud stores carbon for centuries.
• An acre traps as much carbon as a forest.
• Draining the marsh releases it all at once.

The student wants to stress the marsh's value for storing carbon. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: An acre of salt marsh can trap as much carbon as a forest, locking it away in mud for centuries unless the marsh is drained.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to stress the marsh's value for storing carbon.

choices.text: When a salt marsh is drained for development, the carbon it once held is released back into the air all at once.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the marsh's value for storing carbon as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A salt marsh, a coastal wetland flooded by the tides, supports crabs, fish, and a wide variety of wading birds.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the marsh's value for storing carbon as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The dark mud beneath a healthy salt marsh is capable of holding on to stored carbon for many hundreds of years.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the marsh's value for storing carbon as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to stress the marsh's value for storing carbon; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a desert solar tower, a student took the following notes:
• Mirrors focus sunlight on a central tower.
• Molten salt stores the heat for nightfall.
• The plant supplies power after dark.

The student wants to explain how the plant produces power at night. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: By storing the sun's heat in molten salt, the tower keeps supplying power well after dark, when ordinary solar panels go quiet.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain how the plant produces power at night.

choices.text: A wide field of mirrors surrounds the plant, each one angled to focus sunlight onto the tall central tower.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the plant produces power at night as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The tower at the heart of the plant is the point where all of the mirrors direct their concentrated sunlight.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the plant produces power at night as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Solar power, drawn from the energy of the sun, can be captured in several different ways across the world.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the plant produces power at night as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to explain how the plant produces power at night; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a vertical farm, a student took the following notes:
• The farm grows lettuce in stacked indoor trays.
• It uses 95% less water than a field.
• It produces greens year-round in any weather.

The student wants to emphasize the water savings. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: By growing lettuce in stacked indoor trays, the farm produces year-round greens while using 95% less water than a field.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to emphasize the water savings.

choices.text: Because it operates entirely indoors, the vertical farm can produce fresh greens during every season of the year.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the water savings as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The farm relies on stacked trays of lettuce arranged in tall rows inside a carefully controlled indoor space.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the water savings as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Vertical farming, a method of growing crops in stacked layers, is being tried in cities short on open land.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the water savings as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to emphasize the water savings; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a citizen science app, a student took the following notes:
• Users photograph wild plants and animals.
• Experts verify each submitted identification.
• The data helped map a rare frog's range.

The student wants to show how the app advanced real research. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Every identification that a user submits through the app is later checked and verified by a qualified expert.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not show how the app advanced real research as the prompt asks.

choices.text: After experts verified the photos that users submitted, the app's data helped scientists map the full range of a rare frog.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to show how the app advanced real research.

choices.text: Citizen science, in which volunteers help gather data, lets researchers collect far more observations than usual.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not show how the app advanced real research as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Using the app, ordinary people across the region photograph the wild plants and animals they happen to encounter.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not show how the app advanced real research as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to show how the app advanced real research; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a river otter comeback, a student took the following notes:
• Otters vanished from the river by 1970.
• Cleaner water let fish populations rebound.
• Otters returned on their own by 2015.

The student wants to link the otters' return to the cleaner water. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: By the year 1970, river otters had disappeared completely from the waterway that had once been their home.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not link the otters' return to the cleaner water as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A river otter, a sleek and playful mammal, hunts fish and crayfish along the banks of streams and rivers.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not link the otters' return to the cleaner water as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Wildlife officials were pleased to confirm that otters had come back to the river entirely on their own by 2015.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not link the otters' return to the cleaner water as the prompt asks.

choices.text: As cleaner water allowed fish populations to rebound, the otters that had vanished by 1970 returned to the river on their own by 2015.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to link the otters' return to the cleaner water.

explanation: The goal is to link the otters' return to the cleaner water; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a deep-sea expedition, a student took the following notes:
• A submersible reached 6,000 meters down.
• It filmed creatures never seen before.
• Crushing pressure required a titanium hull.

The student wants to emphasize the difficulty of the conditions. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: A submersible, a small craft designed for underwater travel, can carry researchers far deeper than ordinary divers go.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the difficulty of the conditions as the prompt asks.

choices.text: During the dive, the expedition's cameras captured footage of several deep-sea creatures never before seen by anyone.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the difficulty of the conditions as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The submersible used on the expedition was built with a strong titanium hull to keep its crew safe below the surface.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the difficulty of the conditions as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Withstanding crushing pressure that demanded a titanium hull, the submersible descended 6,000 meters to film creatures never seen before.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to emphasize the difficulty of the conditions.

explanation: The goal is to emphasize the difficulty of the conditions; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a freshwater turtle study, a student took the following notes:
• Females nest on the same beach for life.
• Warmer sand produces more female hatchlings.
• Researchers tagged 200 turtles over five years.

The student wants to highlight the link between temperature and sex. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: The female turtles in the study return to nest on the very same stretch of beach throughout their entire lives.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the link between temperature and sex as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Because warmer sand produces more female hatchlings, the temperature of a nesting beach can tip a turtle population toward females.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to highlight the link between temperature and sex.

choices.text: Over the course of five years, the patient researchers managed to tag and track a total of two hundred turtles.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the link between temperature and sex as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A turtle, a reptile protected by a hard shell, can live for many decades in the right freshwater habitat.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the link between temperature and sex as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to highlight the link between temperature and sex; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a language preservation effort, a student took the following notes:
• Only 200 fluent speakers of the language remain.
• A school began teaching it to children in 2020.
• Enrollment has grown each year since.

The student wants to convey both the threat and the response. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: With only about 200 fluent speakers still living, the language is in real danger of disappearing within a generation.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey both the threat and the response as the prompt asks.

choices.text: In 2020 a school began teaching the language to children, and the number enrolling in those classes has grown each year.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey both the threat and the response as the prompt asks.

choices.text: With only 200 fluent speakers left, a school began teaching the language to children in 2020, and enrollment has grown every year since.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to convey both the threat and the response.

choices.text: A language, which is a shared system that lets a community communicate, can fade once its fluent speakers grow few.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey both the threat and the response as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to convey both the threat and the response; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a 3D-printed housing project, a student took the following notes:
• A printer builds each wall in under 24 hours.
• Material costs are roughly half those of brick.
• The first ten homes were occupied this year.

The student wants to emphasize speed and cost. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Using a large printer, crews are able to build each individual wall of a house in well under twenty-four hours.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize speed and cost as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Printing each wall in under a day and at roughly half the material cost of brick, the project delivered its first ten homes this year.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to emphasize speed and cost.

choices.text: The very first ten homes built by the project were finished and occupied by families earlier this year.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize speed and cost as the prompt asks.

choices.text: 3D printing, which forms objects by adding material layer upon layer, is now being applied to houses as well.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize speed and cost as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to emphasize speed and cost; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a hummingbird study, a student took the following notes:
• Hummingbirds beat their wings up to 80 times per second.
• They have the fastest metabolism of any bird.
• A researcher recorded their heart rates in the wild.

The student wants to emphasize the birds' extreme physiology. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Hummingbirds, which a researcher studied in the wild, are among the many bird species that beat their wings to stay aloft.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the birds' extreme physiology as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Although hummingbirds beat their wings quickly, the study focused mainly on how a researcher recorded their heart rates outdoors.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the birds' extreme physiology as the prompt asks.

choices.text: With wings beating up to 80 times per second and the fastest metabolism of any bird, hummingbirds push avian physiology to its limits.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to emphasize the birds' extreme physiology.

choices.text: A researcher who recorded hummingbirds' heart rates in the wild found that they have the fastest metabolism of any bird.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the birds' extreme physiology as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to emphasize the birds' extreme physiology; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.
